title: Melicope cornuta
taxonomy: Melicope cornuta is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification is due to the plant's similarities in leaf and flower morphology to other members of the Rutaceae family. The genus Melicope itself is a part of this family, which comprises various plant species native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia. As a species within this genus, Melicope cornuta shares characteristics with its relatives, such as small white flowers and aromatic leaves. The plant's classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Rutaceae family.

intro: Melicope cornuta, also known as the Mountain Barberry, is a small shrub native to Hawaii and the Pacific Islands, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and red berries. The health benefits of Melicope cornuta include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
